steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The preceding sentence states that the Kármán line is the height where aircraft can no longer remain aloft by lift. The following sentence describes a substitute mechanism — velocity. The blank must signal that something different replaces what came before.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Regardless,’: Signals that the following information holds true despite what was just stated. The second sentence does not contradict or persist against the first — it describes what happens next, not what happens in spite of the first. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘For instance,’: Signals that the second sentence is an example of the first. The second sentence is not an example of lift failing; it describes a different mechanism that takes over. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Granted,’: Signals a reluctant concession. The second sentence is a straightforward description, not a concession to a counterargument. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Instead,’: Signals substitution. Past the Kármán line, velocity replaces lift as the means of sustaining flight — a direct swap of one mechanism for another. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
